finding: `mcpStore.serverDefaultApprovalMode` read a NON-FUNCTION prop off the store proxy inside `onConversationLoad`, an async non-component function. store-kit.ts:283-286 resolves such a prop through `useStore` — a React hook — so this was an invalid hook call on every conversation load. The sibling read two lines below already uses `Stores.McpServer.$` for exactly this reason.
resolution: fixed — hoisted to `Stores.McpComposer.$.serverDefaultApprovalMode` once at the top of the hook, with a comment stating the actions-vs-state rule

finding: The sibling tri-state field `auto_approved_tools` bound `serde_json::Value::Null` for the absent case. That encodes as the JSON value `null`, NOT SQL NULL, so `COALESCE($4, mcp_settings.auto_approved_tools)` always took the first arm and OVERWROTE the stored allow-list; `get_auto_approved_tools`'s `unwrap_or_default()` then rendered it as `[]`. The documented 'None = preserve existing DB value' contract never held, so any save omitting the field destroyed a user's per-tool approvals.
resolution: fixed (ITEM-14) — bind `Option<serde_json::Value>` so None is a real SQL NULL, in both upserts; caught by TEST-14 failing on its first run

finding: Doc comments on OpenAPI-exposed types flow through to `ui/src/api-client/types.ts` as JSDoc. The first draft put ~25 lines of internal backend guidance (branch-divergence policy, migration rationale, sqlx binding notes) on `ApprovalMode` and both request fields, so the generated TS client carried backend implementation detail no frontend reader can act on.
resolution: fixed — one-line client-facing `///` doc on each; internal rationale moved to plain `//` comments, which schemars does not emit. The regenerated types.ts delta is now three small client-relevant hunks.

finding: Adding a REQUIRED `default_approval_mode` to `UserMcpDefaultsGetResponse` broke `tsc` on the gallery mock-API cassette, whose recorded `Mcp.getDefaults` entry was `{}`. A required response field is a contract change every recorded fixture must satisfy.
resolution: fixed — recorded a realistic response, and moved the endpoint into the generator's existing documented LOOSE set (a JSON import widens an enum to `string`; every other enum-carrying recorded endpoint is already there). The ajv contract test remains its gate.

finding: `saveProjectConfig` already captures `const state = get()` at the top, but the new `approval_mode` line called `get()` a second time for one field — a redundant snapshot and an inconsistency with every other read in the same function.

The spec picked the model by the literal 'gpt-4', relying on `createModelViaAPI`'s default display name. That default is env-overridable (`ZIEE_TEST_LLM_MODEL` / `OPENAI_MODEL`), so on a box configured for a local bridge the model picker could silently fail to match and the test would fail for a reason unrelated to what it asserts.
resolution: fixed — the spec creates the model with its OWN display name constant and selects by that

finding: `APPROVAL_MODE_LABEL` duplicates the user-visible option copy from McpConfigModal.tsx:413-426, so a copy change there breaks TEST-22.
resolution: kept — asserting the RENDERED text is the point of TEST-22 (a store assertion cannot prove what the user is shown, per rule B7). The coupling is one map of three strings and fails loudly, not silently.

finding: Checked whether the change can ever LOOSEN the approval gate on khoi/main. It cannot: `ApprovalMode::default()` is `ManualApprove` there, byte-identical to every literal it replaces; the new COALESCE only ever PRESERVES a stored value or applies that same default on insert. On deploy-schedule the default is AutoApprove, which turn 1 already applied — the change makes turn 2+ match turn 1, which is the deployment's configured posture, not a new grant. An explicit user `manual_approve` still wins (TEST-12/13) and a per-tool allow-list still wins (TEST-14/23).
resolution: no defect — behaviour is provably unchanged on khoi and intentional on deploy; pinned by tests rather than left to inspection

finding: Investigated whether promoting the previously-DEAD `ApprovalMode::default()` into a load-bearing value silently changes any other consumer when the deploy branch flips the `#[default]` variant. Grepped every `ApprovalMode` use site: no `unwrap_or_default()` on an ApprovalMode existed before this diff, and none of the six structs holding an `approval_mode` field derives `Default`. `#[serde(default)]` on the new `Option<ApprovalMode>` yields None, not Some(default).
resolution: no defect — the blast radius is exactly the four sites deliberately routed through it; pinned by TEST-1/2/3/4

finding: Checked the read-modify-write risk of the new preserve semantics under concurrent PUTs (e.g. the turn-1 auto-persist racing a modal save). Both upserts remain a SINGLE `INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` statement; the COALESCE reads the conflicting row inside that statement, so there is no read-then-write window and no in-memory merge to lose an update.
resolution: no defect — preserve semantics are evaluated atomically by Postgres

finding: Verified the `Option<Value>` refactor of `auto_approved_tools_json` keeps the serialization failure path: `serde_json::to_value(...)` is still `?`-propagated into an AppError inside the Some arm, rather than being swallowed into None (which would silently write SQL NULL and preserve stale data on a serialization bug).
resolution: no defect — the `?` is inside `Some(...)`, so a serialize error still fails the request

finding: `DEFAULT_APPROVAL_MODE: &str` became `fn default_approval_mode() -> String` because `to_string()` is not const-evaluable. Checked this matches local convention rather than introducing a new shape: the same file already carries a private free helper `fn chrono_from_ts` at module scope, and both call sites already built owned Strings.
resolution: no defect — conforms to the file's existing helper pattern (DEC-3)

finding: Assessed whether `resolve_approval` closes a seam. It is a private free function with a pure signature over the two row types; a future fourth precedence source (e.g. a group-level default) is one added branch in one place, versus the previous inline block that also had to be duplicated for the run_js gate. Its output already feeds BOTH the MCP gate and `execute_run_js_call` (mcp.rs:682), so the two provably cannot drift.
resolution: no defect — strictly more extensible than the inline block it replaces

finding: Checked the new frontend module does not couple the store to React or the ApiClient: it imports only types from `@/api-client/types`, exports pure functions, and is importable by the node:test runner without the store. Mirrors the sibling `approvalRouting.ts` extraction it was modelled on.

`blankMcpConfig` allocates a fresh Map plus two arrays per call. Checked the call sites: all are config-CREATION paths (a pending config, a per-pane seed, a loop-settings first touch), never a render or a per-frame handler, so the allocation is bounded by user actions.
resolution: no defect — returning fresh collections is required to avoid aliasing between configs (pinned by TEST-8)

finding: Checked the modal change for accessibility impact: it alters only the VALUE passed to an existing `Select`, adds no element, no conditional render, and no new interactive control. The accessible name still comes from the selected option's label. No new render branch means no new hook-count path either.
resolution: no defect — value-only change to an existing control
